steps.title: Use the circle form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Substitute the given center
steps.content:
The center is $(14,19)$, so the left side is
$$(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content:
The radius is $8k$, so the right side is
$$(8k)^2=64k^2$$
The equation is $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=64k^2$. The answer is B.
